Title: The genetic basis of replicated bullseye pattern reduction across the Hibiscus trionum complex

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: petal pigmentation involved in bullseye floral patterning and likely pollinator attraction
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: anthocyanin branch competing with flavonol branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reduces dihydroflavonols to leucoanthocyanidins for anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point supported in petals but not formally established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: developmental expression profiling; transgenic overexpression; transgene-induced knockdown/silencing; anthocyanin and flavonol quantification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes anthocyanin accumulation and bullseye pigmentation; knockdown abolishes pigmented bullseye and increases flavonols
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: strongly and preferentially expressed in proximal petal throughout development, 8- to 11-fold higher than distal region
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highest in proximal petal primordia and bullseye region
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HtDFR1 was the major DFR paralog expressed in proximal petals of H. trionum CUBG. 2. Overexpression caused ectopic distal anthocyanin pigmentation, while transgene-induced knockdown nearly abolished petal anthocyanin. 3. Reduced HtDFR1 activity shifted flux toward higher flavonol accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful target to manipulate floral anthocyanin intensity and bullseye size; likely most effective when combined with upstream regulators such as BERRY1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of H. trionum stage 1 petals, GenBank SRA SAMN47793371-SAMN47793390
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: increased anthocyanin can reduce flavonols; knockdown strongly decreases pigmentation and alters branch flux
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: white-flower KD lines likely arose through transgene-triggered silencing rather than designed knockout

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ortholog isolation; coding-sequence comparison; developmental expression profiling correlated with low anthocyanin phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: strongly reduced expression correlates with reduced anthocyanin synthesis and smaller bullseye in H. richardsonii
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: 17- to 107-fold lower in proximal petals and 2- to 9-fold lower in distal petals than HtDFR1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HrDFR1 protein sequence was unchanged relative to HtDFR1. 2. HrDFR1 expression was drastically reduced throughout petal development. 3. Lower expression, rather than coding change, explains reduced anthocyanin synthesis in H. richardsonii.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: marker of reduced floral anthocyanin output; restoration would likely require upstream regulator repair rather than DFR coding change
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR; coding sequence deposited as GenBank PQ450317
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: reduced anthocyanin likely increases flux to flavonols
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: expression reduction is interpreted as downstream of nonfunctional HrBERRY1

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: flavonol branch competing with anthocyanin branch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key branch enzyme for distal flavonol accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: developmental expression profiling; comparative correlation with flavonol distribution; downstream response in HtCREAM1 and HtBERRY1 overexpression backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes flavonol accumulation in distal petals and opposes anthocyanin-biased flux
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in early distal petal stages, 4- to 19-fold higher distally than proximally
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: preferentially distal during early petal development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HtFLS2 was the predominant FLS paralog in petals. 2. Its distal-biased expression mirrored flavonol accumulation. 3. HtCREAM1 increased HtFLS2 expression, whereas HtBERRY1 overexpression reduced HtFLS2 expression distally.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for shifting flavonoid branch allocation toward flavonols versus anthocyanins in petals
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of H. trionum stage 1 petals, GenBank SRA SAMN47793371-SAMN47793390
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: increased FLS2 activity may reduce anthocyanin accumulation through precursor competition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: function inferred from expression and regulatory perturbation; no direct transgenic FLS2 manipulation reported

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: distal petal, also elevated relative flavonol content in petals
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ortholog isolation; coding-sequence comparison; developmental expression profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: maintains distal-biased flavonol branch expression in H. richardsonii; similar profile to HtFLS2 while anthocyanin branch is reduced
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: higher in distal than proximal petal, similar to HtFLS2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HrFLS2 amino acid sequence matched HtFLS2. 2. Its distal-biased expression profile was retained in H. richardsonii. 3. Reduced bullseye pigmentation was therefore linked to anthocyanin-branch suppression rather than FLS2 gain-of-function.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful comparative marker for branch balance in flavonoid engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR; coding sequence deposited as GenBank PQ450318
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: higher relative flavonol flux may accompany reduced anthocyanin
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: not itself implicated as causal locus for bullseye reduction

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: petal bullseye pigmentation associated with floral display and pollinator preference
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: proximal petal bullseye and ovary when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: anthocyanin biosynthesis and branch selection within flavonoid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of anthocyanin pathway genes with probable repression, direct or indirect, of competing flavonol regulators
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates HtDFR1 and HtBERRY2; reduces distal HtCREAM1 and HtFLS2 expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major switch controlling proximal anthocyanin production and bullseye formation; repeated evolutionary target for bullseye reduction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; qRT-PCR developmental profiling; constitutive overexpression; metabolite profiling; F2 segregation/genotyping of orthologous alleles
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes anthocyanin accumulation and expands pigmentation when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: strongly enriched in proximal petals, peaks around stage 3 then declines
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: likely acts in MBW complexes with bHLH and WD40 partners, though partners were not directly validated
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HtBERRY1 is the main proximal MYB regulator associated with bullseye anthocyanin production. 2. Overexpression caused ectopic distal anthocyanin accumulation and increased HtDFR1 transcription. 3. The BERRY1 locus repeatedly underlies evolutionary reduction of pigmented bullseyes across the Trionum complex.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: high-value regulator for engineering petal pigment patterns and anthocyanin distribution; strong candidate for marker-assisted selection or editing of floral display traits
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: enhanced anthocyanin can reduce flavonol levels in some petal regions; pattern may remain structurally visible even when pigmentation expands
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of H. trionum stage 1 petals, GenBank SRA SAMN47793371-SAMN47793390
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: authors propose HtBERRY1 may repress HtCREAM1/HtFLS2 directly or indirectly via an unidentified inhibitor of CREAM1 (IOC)

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: loss of transcription factor function due to very low expression and truncating mutations
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: loss of activator activity correlates with greatly reduced HrDFR1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: causal loss-of-function locus for reduced bullseye pigmentation in H. richardsonii
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ortholog isolation and sequencing; expression profiling; allele-specific genotyping in F2 segregants; phenotype-genotype co-segregation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: nonfunctional HrBERRY1 reduces anthocyanin synthesis and shrinks pigmented bullseye
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: very low in proximal petal development
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: likely defective MBW participation because encoded proteins are truncated
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HrBERRY1 carried two premature stop codons and a large intronic insertion. 2. HrBERRY1 transcripts were detected at extremely low levels. 3. The HrBERRY1 allele perfectly co-segregated with the reduced-bullseye phenotype in the F2 population.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: diagnostic marker for reduced bullseye trait; restoring functional BERRY1 should recover anthocyanin patterning
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss of floral anthocyanin may alter pollinator behavior and shift flavonoid balance toward flavonols
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: coding sequence deposited as GenBank PQ456077
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: authors conclude BERRY1 loss-of-function is very likely causal, though a tightly linked locus cannot be absolutely excluded

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: population 2/4 red-bullseye populations; absent in population 3/5
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB subgroup 6 inferred from orthology to BERRY1
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: presence/absence of regulator gene determines ability to produce red bullseye
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: presence of HvBERRY1 is a perfect predictor of pigmented bullseye in tested F2 progeny
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PCR amplification across natural populations; F1/F2 genetic crossing; blind genotyping and co-segregation analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: presence promotes red bullseye pigmentation; gene loss is associated with absence of pigmented bullseye
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: likely MBW regulator by orthology, not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HvBERRY1 was amplified from red-bullseye populations but not from pale/nonpigmented populations. 2. In an F2 population, HvBERRY1 presence perfectly matched pigmented bullseye presence. 3. Independent loss of BERRY1 activity explains replicated bullseye reduction in H. verdcourtii.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful marker for selecting red-bullseye versus pale-bullseye floral types in natural or breeding populations
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss of pigmentation may affect pollinator interactions
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: coding sequence deposited as GenBank PQ450319
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: in this lineage the causal change appears to be gene absence rather than coding-sequence truncation

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: distal petal flavonol-associated coloration background; antagonizes anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: flavonol branch promotion and overall flavonoid pathway modulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of HtFLS2 with negative effect on anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes HtFLS2 transcription in distal petals
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: distal-biased regulator shaping branch allocation away from anthocyanin-rich bullseye
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; qRT-PCR developmental profiling; constitutive overexpression; downstream gene expression analysis; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overexpression pales the bullseye by reducing proximal anthocyanin and also lowers flavonol content, suggesting broader dampening of flavonoid pathway
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: preferentially expressed in distal petals at early stages, then drops by stage 3
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HtCREAM1 is distally expressed in early petal development. 2. Overexpression reduced proximal anthocyanin and produced paler bullseyes. 3. HtCREAM1 increased HtFLS2 expression, placing it in the flavonol-promoting branch of the petal network.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate regulator to tune anthocyanin/flavonol partitioning and weaken central bullseye pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: constitutive expression reduced both anthocyanins and flavonols, suggesting possible broad suppression of flavonoid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of H. trionum stage 1 petals, GenBank SRA SAMN47793371-SAMN47793390
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: phylogenetic placement did not clearly predict function because the clade lacked Arabidopsis representatives
